_companion_note: RUNNER-DRAFTED companion for COORDINATOR registration. Do NOT auto-register. The Opus runner (kern/opus-runner-8) does not edit registry/assumptions.jsonl. The coordinator assigns the final sequential ASM id (next free was ASM-2503 at draft time), finalizes owner/pseudonym, runs registry-check, and appends to registry/assumptions.jsonl. Backs bead kernel-of-truth-nf5n step 3 + docs/next/design/f1k-inertness-gate-resolution.md.

proposed_row.claim: F1-K KaE-patch INERT-BY-DEFAULT is toolchain-brittle at the machine-code level but a functional property. MEASURED (runner-7 run-log poc/glm52-probe/f1k-harness/opus-runs/20260717T003208Z, kae-bringup.log): on the GCP Spot VM (AMD EPYC 7B13, Milan/znver3, Ubuntu 22.04 gcc, -O2 -march=native) the KAE-unset per-function objdump equivalence of the KaE-patched glm.c vs pristine FAILS outside the allowed set {moe,layer_forward,main,model_init}: functions [attention, run_serve, tok_load] differ byte-wise and kae_load emits un-inlined with kae_free.part.0 partially outlined (shared 78; patch-added [kae_free.part.0, kae_load]). The IDENTICAL bringup chain PASSES on the control box (Intel, gcc 11.5) AND at -march=znver2 and -march=znver3 there (only the 4 allowed functions differ, patch-added only kae_free.part.0) [MEASURED, runner-7 local-repro]. Mechanism: the patch adds file-scope symbols + env-gated branches, and gcc's global inlining/outlining/scheduling lands the perturbation in neighbouring functions on a different toolchain (the same outlining artifact class PATCH-NOTES documents for run_score/kaed_arm). GATE HIERARCHY (ops-encoded per the Fable resolution memo, no re-freeze; the frozen f1k.json 505165ee carries no objdump obligation): the AUTHORITATIVE inert-by-default gate is FUNCTIONAL — with KAE/KAE_SCORE/KAE_DUMP unset and OMP_NUM_THREADS pinned, the production KaE-patched binary's teacher-forced run_score output must be byte-identical to the pristine (pre-patch) engine's on real weights + identical inputs, preceded by a same-binary determinism pre-check (poc/gcp/f1k_worker.sh; fail-closed ERR_F1K_BRINGUP_FUNC). objdump machine-equivalence is DEMOTED to a patch-shape check: FAIL-CLOSED only at the pinned REFERENCE flags -O2 -march=x86-64-v3 (the exact basis the allowed sets were MEASURED on — ASM-2486 {moe,main}; the gate-0 88/92 {moe,layer_forward,main,model_init}; real-checks.sh's default), ADVISORY-only (never fatal, full diff list logged to GATE/objdump-native-advisory.log) at -O2 -march=native. SCOPE: measured on colibri a78a06fc + kae-add-path.patch 11f8b458; the EPYC-7B13 native spill and the control-box/znver2/znver3/x86-64-v3 PASSes are the only measured toolchain points; extends to no other patch, engine, or CPU.
proposed_row.rationale: Escapes deterministic-codegen toolchain-brittleness (the -march=native codegen spill is a proxy artifact, not a behavioural change) by measuring the direct functional property the science needs (KAE-unset forward/scoring byte-identity on real weights), while keeping the fail-closed reference-flags objdump as a bounded patch-shape regression and preserving the native diff-list for audit. The registered run-time SS2.5/SS7.4 60-item off-concept guard byte-identity VOID re-measures functional inertness fail-closed in the pinned analysis.
proposed_row.backing_ref: poc/glm52-probe/f1k-harness/opus-runs/20260717T003208Z/run-log.jsonl (diagnosis + local-repro entries) + gate-pull/bringup/f1k-gate/kae-bringup.log; docs/next/design/f1k-inertness-gate-resolution.md; poc/gcp/bringup_gcp.sh + poc/gcp/f1k_worker.sh (this ops change); this run-log poc/glm52-probe/f1k-harness/opus-runs/20260717T015601Z/run-log.jsonl (functional-gate result at bring-up).
proposed_row.resolution_path: The on-box functional-gate result of this re-launch (poc/gcp/f1k_worker.sh functional inert-by-default gate) provides the direct byte-identity measurement; a native-flags-objdump-still-fails-at-x86-64-v3-on-the-VM outcome would additionally motivate moving the reference objdump pass off-box-only (never widening the allowed set).
